build.title: 1 · The alignment block
build.caption: Two aligned species, alignment gaps stripped: S0 = ACGAC (blue), S1 = ACGTC (orange). This is one MSA block.

build.title: 2 · Concatenate with distinct separators
build.caption: Join the rows into one text T, each ended by its own distinct separator ($₀ < $₁ < A < C < G < T). The separators are smaller than any DNA base, so a DNA pattern can never cross one.

build.title: 3 · Form every cyclic rotation
build.caption: Write out all 12 cyclic rotations of T (here unsorted). Distinct separators make every rotation unique.

build.title: 4 · Sort the rotations → the BWT matrix
build.caption: Sort the rotations lexicographically. This sorted order IS the Wheeler order (Gagie–Manzini–Sirén): the suffix-array rank.

build.title: 5 · Read off the index
build.caption: The last column is the BWT. Each row also yields SA (its text position = POSITION), DOC (the species of that position = SPECIES), and a sampled subset of SA. These four arrays are the whole index.

steps.caption: Prepend A: lo = C[A]+rank(A,0) = 2+0 = 2; hi = C[A]+rank(A,12) = 2+3 = 5. Interval [2,5) now spells “A”.
